description: 'tupeka' is a weight, demand, or difficult responsibility borne by a living being. The same demand may be 'takori' (load) when a technical system is under study or 'ritako' (workload) when work accumulates across time; 'tupeka' keeps the affected person or animal at the center of the account. Another sentence can say whether the bearer chose it and how others share it.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the upper teeth for dental 't' and round into high-back 'u'. Close the lips for 'p', release into stressed mid-front 'e', then close at the soft palate for 'k' and finish with open 'a'.

examples.phi: ta kalora ta whalo tupeka to kolua.
examples.translation: A horse carried a great burden.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The word locates a difficult demand on the one who bears it instead of hiding that experience inside an impersonal account of efficiency.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Harnesses, poles, roads, and wheels changed how weight rested on people and working animals, so the bearer remained part of practical knowledge.

semantic_domains.activity: a difficult demand borne through action or responsibility
semantic_domains.community: weight or demand understood through its effect on a living bearer
semantic_domains.physical: a weight supported by a living body
